books.title: A Woman of Thirty
books.author: Honoré de Balzac
books.summary: The story of a young woman who marries badly, told in several vignettes covering the remainder of her life.

books.title: After London
books.author: Richard Jefferies
books.summary: A young man tries to understand his place in the world as he explores a post-apocalyptic England.

books.title: The Autobiography of Mark Twain
books.author: Mark Twain
books.summary: A collection of reminiscences and reflections from the author’s experiences.

books.title: Tombstone
books.author: Walter Noble Burns
books.summary: An embellished retelling of the town of Tombstone, Arizona, during its heyday in the late 19th century, featuring famous heroes and outlaws of the American Old West.

books.title: The Phoenix and the Carpet
books.author: E. Nesbit
books.summary: Five children once again encounter an ancient, magical creature and go on adventures around the world with its magic carpet.

books.title: The Cords of Vanity
books.author: James Branch Cabell
books.summary: An arrogant young author makes his way through a series of love affairs, avoiding every situation that may cause unpleasantness.

books.title: Henry IV, Part II
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.summary: The rebel forces plan another assault against King Henry’s forces after losing at the Battle of Shrewsbury.

books.title: The Princess and Curdie
books.author: George MacDonald
books.summary: A young miner accepts a mysterious commission that will test his faithfulness and restore justice to a faltering kingdom.

books.title: Oil!
books.author: Upton Sinclair
books.summary: A wealthy oil tycoon’s son grapples with his father’s unethical business practices as he forms alliances with oilfield workers and socialists in Southern California.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: Manly Wade Wellman
books.summary: A collection of horror and science fiction stories by Manly Wade Wellman.

books.title: South!
books.author: Ernest Shackleton
books.summary: A British expedition sets off to cross Antarctica, but when their ship is wrecked they have to face a long struggle for survival.

books.title: The Footsteps at the Lock
books.author: Ronald A. Knox
books.summary: When an unsavory pair of cousins goes missing from their canoe holiday on the Thames, detectives are called in to solve the mystery.

books.title: The Origin of Species
books.author: Charles Darwin
books.summary: A distinguished amateur scientist lays out the evidence for the origin of species by means of natural selection.

books.title: The Everlasting Man
books.author: G. K. Chesterton
books.summary: G. K. Chesterton takes us through the history of man, rebuking common secular views and presenting the case for the Catholic Church.

books.title: Poetry
books.author: Langston Hughes
books.summary: A collection of poems by Langston Hughes.

books.title: Point Counter Point
books.author: Aldous Huxley
books.summary: Several connected stories are interwoven to create a satirical look at English society.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: Henry Kuttner
books.summary: A collection of short fiction by Henry Kuttner, ordered by date of original publication.

books.title: Ukridge Stories
books.author: P. G. Wodehouse
books.summary: A collection of stories featuring P. G. Wodehouse’s charismatic character Stanley Featherstonehaugh Ukridge.

books.title: The City of God
books.author: Augustine of Hippo
books.summary: Bishop Augustine of Hippo provides a defense of the Christian faith against its pagan critics, as well as explaining God’s work and purposes in world history, from beginning to end.

books.title: On a Chinese Screen
books.author: W. Somerset Maugham
books.summary: A collection of short vignettes about the people and places W. Somerset Maugham encountered during his travels along the Yangtze River in 1919–20.
